text.title: Self-Healing Locators with Mandatory Audit Diff Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding the test-self-healing-locators-audited methodology plus the `skip-this-methodology` safety rule. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: A healed candidate MUST share the original locator's WAI-ARIA role AND accessible name. Mismatch on either dimension rejects the heal.
rule.rationale: CSS-only heals click buttons with matching text but different roles; role+name is the smallest pair that prevents wrong-button clicks.

rule.statement: Every heal MUST be emitted as a diff in `healed-selectors.diff` (or platform equivalent). CI MUST NOT auto-apply heals to the next run without a human-merged diff.
rule.rationale: Silent healing makes tests look stable while they drift; the diff is what makes drift visible.

rule.statement: Selectors on payment, auth, or PII screens MUST be blocklisted from auto-healing entirely. Failures on those screens require a human triage.
rule.rationale: A wrong-button click on `Pay` is unrecoverable; healing must not be allowed to take that risk.

rule.statement: A merged heal MUST be reversible within 7 days via a single CLI command pointing at the heal-diff commit.
rule.rationale: Healing introduces silent behavior changes; without a one-shot rollback the team cannot recover when a heal turns out wrong post-merge.

rule.statement: Healing MUST be scoped to selectors that broke since the last successful run; healing every selector every run is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Whole-suite healing introduces drift even on stable selectors and floods the heal-diff with noise.

rule.statement: Skip this methodology when the preconditions in `## Applies If` are not all true, or any item in `## Skip If` holds. The decision tree routes to this rule on those branches.
rule.rationale: A methodology that runs on the wrong input wastes tokens and produces misleading artifacts. The explicit skip rule is the safety valve.
